target.title: [codex] Harden Slack delivery retries
target.body:
## Summary
- retry Slack chat.postMessage delivery on short-lived socket/write failures
- avoid treating queued replies as delivered when the dispatcher recorded delivery failures
- add focused coverage for transient send retry and failed delivery accounting

## Root Cause
Slack turn completion could infer user-facing delivery from queued reply counts even when delivery failed asynchronously. Slack write calls also returned immediately on transient socket readiness errors, which can happen during reconnect windows.

## Validation
- pnpm exec vitest run extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/slack/src/send.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig tsconfig.oxlint.extensions.json extensions/slack/src/send.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts

No credentials, operational logs, or local runtime configuration are included.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Slack delivery retry/accounting path for transient Slack client-readiness failures after the branch was rebased onto current `origin/main`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-pr-fix` on macOS Darwin 25.4.0 arm64 with Node v22.22.2 and pnpm 10.33.2; branch `codex/slack-delivery-retry`, head `c1f8a20fc6d7`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prefer-offline`; `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.channels.config.ts extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit --pretty false`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/slack/src/send.ts extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output from the local real checkout:
  ```text
  pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prefer-offline
  Lockfile is up to date, resolution step is skipped
  Done in 8.6s using pnpm v10.33.2

  pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.channels.config.ts extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts
  [channels shard completed successfully for the two Slack delivery test files]

  pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit --pretty false
  [completed with exit code 0]

  node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/slack/src/send.ts extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts
  Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
  Finished in 2.0s on 4 files with 213 rules using 1 threads.
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The branch rebased cleanly onto current `origin/main`, the Slack delivery-focused validation completed with exit code 0, and GitHub reports the PR head `c1f8a20fc6d7` as `mergeable=MERGEABLE`.
- What was not tested: Live Slack workspace delivery was not exercised; the proof above is from a local OpenClaw source checkout with redacted terminal output.
